name: Container Performance Monitoring
description: Monitor CPU, memory, network, and I/O usage of running containers to identify performance bottlenecks.

steps.title: View real-time stats for all containers
steps.description: Monitor resource usage across all running containers.

steps.title: Get a single snapshot of stats
steps.description: Capture a one-time snapshot without streaming for scripts and logging.

steps.title: Inspect container resource limits
steps.description: Check what resource limits are set for a container.
steps.tip: Memory is in bytes. Divide by 1048576 to get MB. CpuQuota of 100000 = 1 CPU core.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Container Performance Monitoring
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Container Resource Usage ==="
docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Name}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}\t{{.MemUsage}}\t{{.MemPerc}}\t{{.NetIO}}\t{{.BlockIO}}'

echo ""
echo "=== Container Uptime ==="
docker ps --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.RunningFor}}'

faq.question: How do I set memory limits on a container?
faq.answer: Use the -m or --memory flag: 'docker run -m 512m myapp'. In Compose, set 'deploy.resources.limits.memory: 512M'.

faq.question: What does high memory percentage mean?
faq.answer: If a container is consistently above 80% of its memory limit, it risks being OOM-killed. Increase the limit or optimize the application's memory usage.
